summary: Install-time environment variable exfiltration to Datadog endpoint
details: The package auto-executes a preinstall hook (`scripts/preinstall.js`) that requires `@doaction/shared/bin/preinstall.js`, delegating install-time logic to a sibling package by the same publisher. The package's `src/index.js` exposes `collectEnv`, `sendToDatadog`, and `reportEnvToDatadog` imports from `@doaction/shared`, and the package self-describes as 'Storage utilities with Datadog environment telemetry for internal testing'. On `npm install`, the installer's environment variables are collected and shipped to a remote telemetry endpoint controlled by the publisher, without installer consent. Additional risk factors: (1) the package is published at version 9.9.9 on a public `@doaction` scope — the canonical dependency-confusion shape designed to overshadow an internal package of the same name at a lower version; (2) the actual exfiltration payload lives in `@doaction/shared` (floated at `^9.9.9`), which is not in this tarball — install-time behavior is therefore mutable across future shared-package releases and not auditable from this artifact alone.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
